title: Höjda ambitioner och nordisk storaffär
summary_md:
# Höjda ambitioner och nordisk storaffär

**Feed**: efn-borsfrukost  
**Published**: 2026-06-16  
**Market Sentiment**: Mixed

⏱️ **Time Horizon Key**: ST = <3 months | MT = 3-12 months | LT = >12 months

---

## Episode Thesis

This episode of Börsfrukost (2026-06-16) centers on a Nordic M&A wave and corporate updates: Norwegian acquiring Nordic Leisure Travel Group (Ving) for SEK 7.9bn, EQT fully exiting Beijer Ref via a placing with MSAB taking over as anchor owner, and Securitas raising its financial ambitions ahead of its capital markets day. The hosts read the broader market as buoyant on hopes of a peace deal, with the Stockholm exchange up and 28 of 30 large caps rising. The tone is mixed-to-positive: constructive on Securitas (cheap) and Beijer Ref (fresh start), but cautious on the airline merger and outright negative on Cloetta's valuation.

---

## Universe Stocks

### 🟢 SECU-B — Securitas | **BUY** (Moderate)

**Sentiment**: Positive | **Time Horizon**: MT  
**Confidence**: 65%

New CMD targets (10% EPS growth, 10% margin, higher cash conversion, lower leverage) on a stock trading ~P/E 12 for a stable, increasingly tech-led business.

#### Reasoning

Hosts view Securitas as cheap: valuation is lower than before the Stanley acquisition despite margins rising from 5% to 8% and a higher tech content. With a credible 10% EPS growth target over the cycle and improving cash conversion plus lower debt (2.5x EBITDA), ~P/E 12 looks inexpensive for a stable, defensive name. They expect only a lukewarm but slightly positive market reaction near-term.

#### Sell Triggers

- if the 10% EPS growth target is not met over the cycle
- if margin progress toward 10% stalls
- if the tech-content shift fails to lift profitability

> *"Tror man på de här målen så är det P12 eller någonting nu. Minst tillväxten på 10% är väldigt billigt för en sån stabil verksamhet också."*

---

### 🔴 CLA-B — Cloetta | **SELL** (Moderate)

**Sentiment**: Negative | **Time Horizon**: MT  
**Confidence**: 60%

Ture's published sell analysis: great operational execution but stock up ~150-200% from the bottom, EV/EBIT ~14.6 looks stretched given only 3-4% growth.

#### Reasoning

Cloetta does everything right operationally and surprised with pick'n'mix profitability (12% margin last quarter vs a 7-9% target), but the shares have run ~200% off the bottom. At EV/EBIT ~14.6 on this year's earnings the valuation is stretched for a business growing only 3-4%, meaning further margin gains are needed to drive earnings growth — which Ture doubts is sustainable, partly as recent pick'n'mix strength was boosted by an early Easter.

#### Sell Triggers

- if pick'n'mix margins normalize back toward the 7-9% target
- if growth stays stuck at 3-4% without further margin expansion
- if the early-Easter boost reverses in later quarters

> *"Jag har svårt att räkna fram någon större uppsida med dagens värdering. Med bakgrund av den här starka utvecklingen som vi har haft."*

---

### 🟠 BEIJ-B — Beijer Ref | **WATCH** (Weak)

**Sentiment**: Positive | **Time Horizon**: LT  
**Confidence**: 50%

EQT overhang removed; MSAB becomes strong anchor owner providing continuity. A fine company getting a fresh start after a period of high debt and slow M&A.

#### Reasoning

With EQT fully exiting, the long-standing ownership overhang is gone and MSAB steps in as a strong anchor owner, restoring continuity. Hosts call it a fine company and a former fund-manager favorite, now with a fresh start after a stretch of high leverage and stalled acquisitions. They also argue it could benefit long-term from greater focus on oil/gas dependence during the Iran conflict, though they downplay the modest placing-discount price move.

#### Sell Triggers

- if acquisition-led growth stays sluggish
- if leverage rises again
- if MSAB fails to provide stable long-term ownership

> *"Det är ett fint bolag. Och de borde ju också kunna gynnas tänker jag. Långsiktigt av att liksom man får syn på det här olje- och gasberoendet under Iran-konflikten."*

---

### 🟠 NAS — Norwegian | **WATCH** (Weak)

**Sentiment**: Mixed | **Time Horizon**: MT  
**Confidence**: 45%

Acquiring Nordic Leisure Travel Group with clear synergies, but airlines are a brutal sector; deal priced ~9x pre-pandemic earnings amid the Hormuz crisis and high fuel costs.

#### Reasoning

Hosts understand the strategic rationale — hotels plus flights with real synergies and scale benefits seen before in airline consolidation — but are far from convinced it will be a blockbuster deal. The sector is extremely tough, and what might have been bought at 4-5x pre-pandemic earnings now costs closer to 9x, with the unresolved Hormuz crisis driving up jet-fuel costs adding risk.

#### Sell Triggers

- if fuel costs stay elevated due to the Hormuz/Iran crisis
- if the acquisition synergies fail to materialize
- if the integration proves too costly

> *"Det är klart att man kan förstå rationalen men det är väl kanske ingenting man är helt säker på. Det blir någon dunderaffär."*

---

| **CLA-B** | Cloetta | 🔴 SELL | MT | 60% | Ture's published sell analysis: great operational execution but stock up ~150-200% from the bottom, EV/EBIT ~14.6 looks stretched given only 3-4% growth. |
| **BEIJ-B** | Beijer Ref | 🟠 WATCH | LT | 50% | EQT overhang removed; MSAB becomes strong anchor owner providing continuity. A fine company getting a fresh start after a period of high debt and slow M&A. |
| **NAS** | Norwegian | 🟠 WATCH | MT | 45% | Acquiring Nordic Leisure Travel Group with clear synergies, but airlines are a brutal sector; deal priced ~9x pre-pandemic earnings amid the Hormuz crisis and high fuel costs. |
